category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: Your price is a bit high for us. / B: If you increase the order ( ), we could offer a 5% discount.
q: If you increase the order ( ), we could offer a 5% discount.
exp: 取引量には volume を使います。条件提示の基本です。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: We need this by Friday. / B: That's tight. We'd expect a ( ) in return.
exp: concession（譲歩）。何かを受け入れる代わりに条件を出す際の重要語。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: Is this your final offer? / B: We might have some ( ) room if you extend the contract.
exp: wiggle room で「交渉の余地／含み」を意味する慣用表現。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: I'm afraid we can't go any lower. / B: Then it seems we've reached an ( ).
q: Then it seems we've reached an ( ).
exp: impasse（行き詰まり、デッドロック）。交渉が難航している状態。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: Let's find a ( ) ground. / B: I agree. Let's look at the terms again.
exp: find a middle ground で「妥協点を見つける」。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: Can you meet us ( ) on the shipping costs? / B: We can cover 50%.
q: Can you meet us ( ) on the shipping costs?
exp: meet halfway で「歩み寄る（折半する）」。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: This term is ( ). / B: We understand. Let's remove it then.
exp: non-negotiable（交渉の余地なし）。譲れない条件を指します。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: What is your ( ) line? / B: We cannot go below $10 per unit.
exp: bottom line で「最低限の条件／最終的な結論」。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: We'll ( ) the fee if you sign today. / B: That's a tempting offer.
exp: waive は「（権利や手数料を）放棄する、免除する」。

category: 01. ネゴシエーション・条件交渉
dialogue: A: Let's ( ) it in writing. / B: I'll send the draft contract.
exp: put it in writing で「書面に残す」。合意の際の必須フレーズ。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: What makes you different? / B: Our main ( ) is our 24/7 support.
exp: differentiator（差別化要因）。競合との違いを強調する語。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: Is it worth the cost? / B: Absolutely. The ( ) on investment is high.
exp: Return on Investment (ROI) 「投資利益率」。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: We need a solution that ( ) our needs. / B: Our software is fully customizable.
q: We need a solution that ( ) our needs.
exp: address one's needs で「ニーズに対応する」。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: How does this help us? / B: It will ( ) your workflow.
exp: streamline（簡素化する、効率化する）。提案での人気単語。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: Can you ( ) the benefits? / B: Certainly. First, it reduces costs...
exp: outline（概要を述べる）。要点を整理して伝える際に使います。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: We are looking for ( ) results. / B: You'll see an improvement in 30 days.
exp: tangible results（具体的な／目に見える成果）。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: Our product has a competitive ( ). / B: Tell me more about your pricing.
exp: competitive edge で「競争優位性」。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: This feature is ( ) for large teams. / B: That sounds perfect for us.
q: This feature is ( ) for large teams.
exp: tailored to/for で「〜に合わせて作られた、特化した」。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: Let me ( ) you through the demo. / B: Please do.
q: Let me ( ) you through the demo.
exp: walk someone through で「〜を順を追って説明する」。

category: 02. 提案・売り込み（ピッチ）
dialogue: A: What's the ( )? / B: You get three months free if you join now.
exp: incentive（動機付け、特典）。購買意欲を高める施策のこと。

dialogue: A: ( ) to my email sent yesterday, I'm waiting for your reply.
exp: Further to... は「〜の件ですが（追伸）」と催促する際の手法。

dialogue: A: We ( ) to inform you that the event is canceled. / B: That's a shame.
exp: 「残念ながら〜をお知らせします」というフォーマルな断り。

dialogue: A: I'm writing ( ) behalf of Mr. Smith. / B: Thank you for reaching out.
q: I'm writing ( ) behalf of Mr. Smith.
exp: on behalf of で「〜を代表して、〜の代わりに」。

dialogue: A: Please ( ) the receipt for your records. / B: Got it.
exp: retain（保持する）。keepよりもフォーマルな事務表現。

dialogue: A: Could you ( ) the receipt of this email? / B: Yes, I'll reply now.
q: Could you ( ) the receipt of this email?
exp: acknowledge receipt で「受領を確認する」。

dialogue: A: Please ( ) the attached document. / B: I'll review it today.
exp: Please find attached... は「添付書類をご確認ください」の定型句。

dialogue: A: Thank you for your ( ) attention to this matter. / B: You're welcome.
exp: prompt（迅速な）。ビジネスメールでの感謝の定番形容詞。

dialogue: A: ( ) to our conversation, I'm sending the files. / B: Thanks.
exp: Following up to で「先ほどの会話の続きですが」。

dialogue: A: Your ( ) in this matter is greatly appreciated. / B: No problem.
q: Your ( ) in this matter is greatly appreciated.
exp: 「ご協力に感謝します」のフォーマルな言い回し。

dialogue: A: I'll ( ) you updated on any changes. / B: Please do.
exp: keep someone updated で「常に最新情報を伝える」。

dialogue: A: How are the sales? / B: They have remained ( ) for three months.
q: They have remained ( ) for three months.
exp: steady（着実な、安定した）。グラフ説明で多用されます。

dialogue: A: The costs ( ) for 40% of the budget. / B: That's a lot.
exp: account for で「〜の割合を占める」。

dialogue: A: There was a ( ) drop in users last week. / B: Was it a server issue?
q: There was a ( ) drop in users.
exp: sharp drop で「急激な下落」。

dialogue: A: Sales ( ) in December due to the holiday. / B: That's expected.
exp: peak（最高潮に達する）。動詞として使われます。

dialogue: A: We need to ( ) the data before the meeting. / B: I'll prepare the report.
exp: analyze（分析する）。

dialogue: A: Profits are ( ) to double next year. / B: That's very optimistic.
exp: projected to で「〜と予測されている」。

dialogue: A: Let's ( ) these two charts. / B: One shows growth, the other shows cost.
exp: compare（比較する）。

dialogue: A: There is a ( ) towards online shopping. / B: Yes, it's growing every year.
q: There is a ( ) towards online shopping.
exp: trend（傾向）。

dialogue: A: The numbers ( ) slightly every month. / B: As long as it's not a big drop, it's fine.
exp: fluctuate（（数値が）変動する、上下する）。

dialogue: A: To ( ) it, our revenue is up by 10%. / B: Good job.
q: To ( ) it, our revenue is up...
exp: summarize（要約する）。

dialogue: A: What's our ( ) B if this fails? / B: We'll revert to the old version.
q: What's our ( ) B if this fails?
exp: Plan B（代替案）。

dialogue: A: We need to ( ) these tasks. / B: I'll start with the most urgent one.
exp: prioritize（優先順位をつける）。

dialogue: A: Let's ( ) all the possible risks. / B: I'll make a list.
exp: evaluate（評価する）。

dialogue: A: What is the ( ) of this delay? / B: It's a lack of staff.
q: What is the ( ) of this delay?
exp: implication（（予想される）影響、含み）。

dialogue: A: We need to ( ) a consensus. / B: Let's vote then.
exp: reach a consensus で「合意に達する」。

dialogue: A: That's a ( ) solution for now. / B: We need a permanent one later.
exp: temporary（一時的な）。

dialogue: A: Let's ( ) the pros and cons. / B: Good idea. Let's start with the pros.
exp: weigh the pros and cons で「メリットとデメリットを比較検討する」。

dialogue: A: We have to ( ) to the original plan. / B: Why? Is there a problem?
q: We have to ( ) to the original plan.
exp: stick to で「（方針などを）変えずに守る」。

dialogue: A: It's a ( ) decision. / B: I know, but we have to choose one.
exp: tough decision（難しい決断）。

dialogue: A: Who has the ( ) say on this? / B: The CEO does.
q: Who has the ( ) say on this?
exp: final say で「最終決定権」。

dialogue: A: I have a proven ( ) record in sales. / B: Impressive. Tell me more.
exp: track record（実績）。

dialogue: A: We are looking for a ( ) for this role. / B: How about Sarah?
q: We are looking for a ( ) for this role.
exp: candidate（候補者）。

dialogue: A: What are your ( )? / B: I'm good at problem-solving and coding.
exp: strengths（強み）。面接の定番質問。

dialogue: A: Do you have any ( ) from your previous job? / B: Yes, my old manager wrote one.
exp: references（推薦状、照会先）。採用で重視されます。

dialogue: A: Your performance has been ( ). / B: Thank you, I worked hard.
exp: outstanding（傑出した、非常に優れた）。

dialogue: A: We need to ( ) new employees. / B: Let's start the interview process.
exp: recruit（採用する、募集する）。

dialogue: A: What is the ( ) for this position? / B: It's $50,000 per year.
q: What is the ( ) for this position?
exp: salary（給与）。

dialogue: A: How do you handle ( )? / B: I try to learn from it and improve.
exp: criticism（批判）。フィードバックへの姿勢を問う質問。

dialogue: A: He was ( ) to senior manager last month. / B: He deserves it.
exp: promote（昇進させる）。

dialogue: A: We provide ( ) for all new staff. / B: That's great for their growth.
q: We provide ( ) for all new staff.
exp: mentorship（メンター制度）。

dialogue: A: Is the project on track? / B: Yes, we hit a major ( ).
exp: milestone（重要な節目）。

dialogue: A: What is the ( ) for this task? / B: It must be finished by Friday.
q: What is the ( ) for this task?
exp: deadline（締め切り）。

dialogue: A: We are ( ) schedule. / B: We need to speed up.
exp: behind schedule（予定より遅れて）。

dialogue: A: Who is ( ) for this part? / B: John is the lead developer.
exp: responsible for で「〜に責任がある、〜の担当である」。

dialogue: A: Let's ( ) the progress in the next meeting. / B: I'll prepare the slides.
exp: review（再検討する、振り返る）。

dialogue: A: We need to allocate more ( ) to this project. / B: I'll talk to the finance team.
exp: resources（リソース、資源）。人・モノ・金を指します。

dialogue: A: The project ( ) has changed. / B: Again? We need more time then.
exp: scope（（業務の）範囲）。

dialogue: A: We are ( ) budget. / B: Great, let's keep it that way.
exp: under budget（予算内で）。

dialogue: A: Can you give me an ( )? / B: Yes, everything is going well.
exp: update（最新状況）。

dialogue: A: Let's ( ) a kickoff meeting. / B: How about next Monday?
exp: hold a meeting で「会議を開く」。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: I'm upset about the delay. / B: We are looking into the ( ) cause.
q: We are looking into the ( ) cause.
exp: root cause（根本原因）。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: I want a refund. / B: Let me check our ( ).
exp: policy（規定、方針）。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: This is not what I ordered. / B: I ( ) for the mistake. We'll replace it.
exp: apologize（謝罪する）。ビジネスでの基本。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: How will you ( ) this? / B: We'll send a technician today.
exp: resolve（解決する）。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: I've been waiting for an hour. / B: Thank you for your ( ). We're almost ready.
exp: patience（忍耐、根気）。待たせた相手への言葉。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: The product is ( ). / B: We'll send a replacement immediately.
exp: defective（欠陥のある）。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: I'm not ( ) with the service. / B: I'm sorry to hear that. How can we improve?
exp: satisfied with で「〜に満足している」。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: Can I speak to the manager? / B: He is ( ) right now. Can I help?
exp: unavailable（対応できない）。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: This is the third time this happened. / B: We take your ( ) very seriously.
exp: feedback（意見、感想）。

category: 08. クレーム処理・顧客対応
dialogue: A: I'll ( ) this to my supervisor. / B: Thank you.
exp: escalate（（上司などに）報告する、段階を引き上げる）。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: His feedback was very ( ). / B: Yes, he avoids direct 'No'.
exp: subtle（微妙な、遠回しな）。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: We need to be ( ) of local customs. / B: True, let's do some research.
q: We need to be ( ) of local customs.
exp: mindful of で「〜に留意している、配慮している」。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: Is there a ( ) barrier? / B: A little, but we use simple English.
exp: language barrier（言葉の壁）。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: They value ( ) over speed. / B: We should not rush the decision then.
exp: harmony（調和、和）。日本などのビジネス文化の特徴。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: What is the ( ) for business attire? / B: Usually, it's business casual.
q: What is the ( ) for business attire?
exp: norm（標準、慣習）。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: We should avoid ( ) during the meeting. / B: Good point, it might be confusing.
q: We should avoid ( ) during the meeting.
exp: jargon（専門用語）。多国籍チームでは避けるべきもの。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: Punctuality is ( ) here. / B: I'll make sure to arrive early.
exp: crucial（極めて重要な）。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: Let's ( ) our expectations. / B: Yes, let's make sure we mean the same thing.
exp: align（一致させる、調整する）。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: This is a ( ) situation for everyone. / B: I agree, nobody wins.
exp: lose-lose（共倒れの）。win-winの反対。

category: 09. 異文化・グローバルビジネス
dialogue: A: We need to ( ) the gap between our offices. / B: Communication is key.
exp: bridge the gap で「溝を埋める、橋渡しをする」。

dialogue: A: I need to do some ( ) to get support. / B: Good luck with the stakeholders.
exp: groundwork（下準備、根回し）。

dialogue: A: I want to ( ) for the manager position. / B: You have my support.
q: I want to ( ) for the manager position.
exp: apply for で「〜に応募する、志願する」。

dialogue: A: She is very ( ). / B: Yes, she wants to be the CEO one day.
exp: ambitious（野心的な）。

dialogue: A: I need to ( ) my skills. / B: Why not take an online course?
exp: upgrade one's skills で「スキルを向上させる」。

dialogue: A: Networking is ( ) for your career. / B: I should attend more events.
exp: essential（不可欠な）。

dialogue: A: He has a lot of ( ) in the company. / B: Everyone listens to him.
q: He has a lot of ( ) in the company.
exp: influence（影響力）。

dialogue: A: I'm looking for a better work-life ( ). / B: Many people are these days.
q: I'm looking for a better work-life ( ).
exp: work-life balance（仕事と生活の調和）。

dialogue: A: You need to ( ) for yourself. / B: I'll ask for a raise tomorrow.
exp: advocate for oneself で「自分の正当性を主張する、自己弁護する」。

dialogue: A: There are many ( ) for growth here. / B: That's why I joined.
exp: opportunities（機会）。

dialogue: A: I'm ( ) a new challenge. / B: Good for you!
exp: seek（追い求める）。
